reasons: Valir can push Lapu-Lapu away when he tries to enter melee range.
Slow and knockback reduce Lapu-Lapu's ability to stick to backline targets.

proof.summary: Lapu-Lapu wants to dive and stay in melee range, while Valir can knock him back, slow him, and reset spacing.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Lapu-Lapu's real dive.
Valir positions behind frontline instead of standing alone.
failureCases: Lapu-Lapu flanks and reaches Valir first.
Valir wastes knockback before the transformation engage.

reasons: Karrie's stacking true damage threatens durable fighters.
Lapu-Lapu's damage-reduction style is less reliable against true-damage pressure.

proof.summary: Lapu-Lapu survives dives through shield and damage reduction, while Karrie can stack max-HP true damage if protected.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and safe spacing.
Lapu-Lapu is forced to fight front-to-back.
failureCases: Karrie is behind in items and cannot apply stacks safely.

reasons: Khufra can punish Lapu-Lapu's mobility and dive path.
Bouncing Ball is useful against heroes that rely on blink or dash movement across the frontline.

proof.summary: Lapu-Lapu uses mobility to enter fights, while Khufra can punish blink-style movement and disrupt his path into the backline.
worksBestWhen: Khufra positions between Lapu-Lapu and the backline.
Khufra times Bouncing Ball for the dive, not poke.
failureCases: Khufra is controlled before he can block the engage.

reasons: Kaja can stop Lapu-Lapu after he commits into the fight.
Suppression creates a controlled burst window against a hero who normally wants extended brawls.

proof.summary: Lapu-Lapu wants to commit with durability and AoE pressure, while Kaja can suppress and drag him out of his preferred fight position.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Lapu-Lapu's full commit.
Teammates save burst for the suppression window.
failureCases: Kaja uses ultimate before Lapu-Lapu transforms.
Lapu-Lapu's team counter-engages during the drag.

reasons: Franco can punish Lapu-Lapu before he gets full teamfight value.
Suppression is useful after Lapu-Lapu dives too deep.

proof.summary: Lapu-Lapu is hardest to handle once fully committed in enhanced stance, while Franco can punish mistimed entries or catch him after key cooldowns.
worksBestWhen: Franco waits until Lapu-Lapu spends mobility.
Team follow-up is ready during Bloody Hunt.
failureCases: Franco suppresses Lapu-Lapu while he is still too tanky and unsupported follow-up is low.
